Description:
Provision Azure Container Apps and AWS ECS Fargate infrastructure via Terraform with CI validate/plan/apply pipeline and Docker image signing.

SCOPE: MVP 2
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure / Cloud
STORIES: 11.1 - 11.4

Create infrastructure/terraform/azure/ with main.tf, variables.tf, and outputs.tf for Azure Container Apps deployment.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Provider: azurerm, version >= 3.90
- Resources: azurerm_resource_group, azurerm_container_app_environment, azurerm_container_app
- Container App: image from ACR, port 80, min_replicas=1, max_replicas=5
- Environment variables sourced from Azure Key Vault references (no secrets in Terraform state)
- Ingress: external, allow HTTP, HTTPS redirect
- outputs.tf: app_url, resource_group_name, acr_login_server
- variables.tf: environment (dev/staging/prod), location, acr_name, app_name, image_tag

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: JD-2

TESTING CRITERIA:
- terraform init, validate, plan all succeed
- terraform apply creates all resources
- App accessible at output app_url
- Container restarts on failure (replica auto-recovery)

EDGE CASES:
- ACR pull credentials must be configured in Container App registry block
- Key Vault references require managed identity assignment

Create infrastructure/terraform/aws/ with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf for AWS ECS Fargate deployment.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Provider: aws, version >= 5.0
- Resources: aws_ecs_cluster, aws_ecs_task_definition, aws_ecs_service, aws_lb, aws_lb_target_group
- Task definition: fargate, cpu=512, memory=1024, port 80
- Service: desired_count=1, assign_public_ip=true
- Secrets sourced from AWS Secrets Manager (no secrets in task definition JSON)
- outputs.tf: lb_dns_name, ecs_cluster_name, task_definition_arn
- variables.tf: environment, aws_region, ecr_image_uri, desired_count

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: JD-2

TESTING CRITERIA:
- terraform init, validate, plan all succeed
- ECS service starts task and registers healthy target
- Load balancer DNS resolves correctly

EDGE CASES:
- Fargate requires awsvpc network mode — security group must allow port 80 from ALB
- ECR image URI must match exact tag (no :latest in production)

Add Terraform validate, plan, and apply steps to CI/CD workflow.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add terraform-validate job to ci.yml: runs on PR for both azure/ and aws/ modules
- terraform init -backend=false for validation (no state needed)
- terraform validate for each module
- terraform plan (with -out=plan.tfplan) on merge to main
- terraform apply plan.tfplan on manual approval (GitHub Environments protection rule)
- Cache .terraform directory in CI to speed up init
- Store plan output as CI artifact

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure / CI
DEPENDENCIES: JD-53, JD-54, JD-31

TESTING CRITERIA:
- terraform validate runs on every PR
- Plan output visible in CI artifact
- Apply requires manual approval gate
- Invalid Terraform blocks fail PR check

EDGE CASES:
- Terraform state backend must be configured before apply (Azure Storage Account)
- Parallel terraform apply to multiple environments must be prevented

Configure GitHub Actions to sign and tag Docker images with Git commit SHA on merge to main.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add docker-build-push job to ci.yml (runs on push to main only)
- Build Docker image using Dockerfile
- Tag as: {registry}/{image-name}:{git-sha} and {registry}/{image-name}:latest
- Push to Azure Container Registry (ACR) for Azure deployment
- Push to AWS ECR for AWS deployment
- Sign image using cosign with GitHub OIDC token (keyless signing)
- Store image digest as CI output for Terraform apply step

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure / CI
DEPENDENCIES: JD-2, JD-55

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Image tagged with exact git SHA (no latest-only)
- cosign signature verifiable
- Image available in ACR and ECR after push
- Terraform apply uses SHA-tagged image (not latest)

EDGE CASES:
- ACR and ECR login credentials stored as GitHub secrets
- cosign requires OIDC token permissions: id-token: write in workflow

Create prompt files for all MVP 2 AI agents: ranking, RAG, cover letter, question-answer, security, and orchestrator.

SCOPE: MVP 2
PROJECT AREA: Prompt Engineering
STORIES: 12.1 - 12.5

Create prompts/ranking-agent/ with CONTRACT.md, CHANGELOG.md, system.md, scoring.md, reranking.md, filtering.md.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- CONTRACT.md: model=claude-sonnet-4-6, reasoning_effort=medium, max_tokens=8000, temperature=0
- system.md: XML prompt for 8-step ranking pipeline with explicit step instructions
- scoring.md: embedding model selection rationale, cosine similarity thresholds, scoring weight table
- reranking.md: cross-encoder model (ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2), confidence threshold, rerank strategy
- filtering.md: post-ranking filter rules (similarity >= 0.75, seniority validation rules)

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompt Engineering
DEPENDENCIES: JD-32, JD-44

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All 6 files present with ranking-specific content
- CONTRACT.md reasoning_effort=medium
- Scoring thresholds match ranking_agent.py implementation

EDGE CASES:
- Prompt update must increment version and update CHANGELOG.md

Create prompts/rag-agent/ with CONTRACT.md, CHANGELOG.md, system.md, retrieval.md, embeddings.md, personalization.md.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- CONTRACT.md: reasoning_effort=high, max_tokens=16000, temperature=0
- system.md: XML prompt for contextual retrieval with explicit source priority order
- retrieval.md: retrieval strategy, chunk size (512 tokens), overlap (50 tokens), top-k=5
- embeddings.md: embedding model selection, dimension (1536), similarity metric (cosine)
- personalization.md: context assembly rules, CV priority, application history weighting

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompt Engineering
DEPENDENCIES: JD-32, JD-45

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All 6 files present
- CONTRACT.md reasoning_effort=high
- Retrieval strategy consistent with rag_agent.py implementation

EDGE CASES:
- Context window overflow documented in retrieval.md with truncation strategy

Create prompts/cover-letter-agent/ with CONTRACT.md, CHANGELOG.md, system.md, tone.md, generation.md, templates.md.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- CONTRACT.md: reasoning_effort=medium, max_tokens=4000, temperature=0
- system.md: XML prompt with 6-section playbook instructions
- tone.md: tone adaptation rules per job type (startup/enterprise/agency), formality levels
- generation.md: ATS keyword injection rules, word count targets (300-500 words), section ordering
- templates.md: three cover letter templates (technical/consulting/AI-focused) with placeholder syntax

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompt Engineering
DEPENDENCIES: JD-32, JD-46

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All 6 files present
- Templates in templates.md use consistent placeholder syntax
- ATS rules in generation.md match cover_letter_agent.py >= 60% threshold

EDGE CASES:
- Tone adaptation must not change factual claims — document constraint in tone.md

Create prompts/question-answer-agent/ and prompts/security-agent/ with system.md and supporting files.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- question-answer-agent/: CONTRACT.md (reasoning_effort=high), CHANGELOG.md, system.md (XML with grounding rules), tools.md (permitted retrieval tools)
- security-agent/: CONTRACT.md (reasoning_effort=high), CHANGELOG.md, system.md (injection detection patterns, OWASP rules, sanitisation instructions)
- Both: temperature=0, explicit I don't know instruction for unanswerable inputs
- security system.md: injection pattern list as XML <patterns> block

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompt Engineering
DEPENDENCIES: JD-32, JD-47, JD-49

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Both agent prompt directories complete with all required files
- Injection patterns in security system.md cover common jailbreak attempts
- Q&A system.md has explicit no-hallucination constraint

EDGE CASES:
- Security agent prompt itself must be injection-resistant (no user input interpolated into system prompt)

Create prompts/orchestrator/ with CONTRACT.md, CHANGELOG.md, and system.md at xhigh reasoning effort.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- CONTRACT.md: model=claude-sonnet-4-6, reasoning_effort=xhigh, max_tokens=64000, temperature=0
- Xhigh reasoning: required for long-horizon multi-step coordination
- system.md: XML prompt with <role> workflow coordinator, <instructions> activity sequencing, retry rules, DLQ routing, checkpoint strategy
- Include explicit rules: never skip activities, always log state transitions, honour circuit breaker signals

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompt Engineering
DEPENDENCIES: JD-32, JD-50

TESTING CRITERIA:
- CONTRACT.md reasoning_effort=xhigh documented
- system.md covers all orchestrator responsibilities
- Rules consistent with orchestrator_agent.py Temporal workflow definition

EDGE CASES:
- xhigh reasoning significantly increases token cost — document expected token budget
